body:
## Summary

Port the finalized auto-running label control from the dangling original-agent PR into Sepo.

- Add `agent-auto-running` as a visible opt-out marker for active orchestrator automation chains.
- Apply the label before the first handoff, require it for later handoffs, and remove it best-effort when the orchestrator stops.
- Route terminal review/fix-pr outcomes through the orchestrator cleanup path so stale labels are removed.
- Skip the agent planner for source outcomes that the built-in policy already treats as terminal.
- Add CLI/workflow regression coverage for label absence, label pagination, first-dispatch labeling, dispatch-failure cleanup, and planner preflight.

Port source commit: `7d6838539c804b72f99792fda8b4540b33fef037`, adapted for Sepo naming and repository slugs.

## Review notes

I reviewed the original PR conversation and current head before porting. The code-level correctness findings had already been addressed upstream:

- stale label cleanup for terminal downstream paths
- paginated label reads
- avoiding later-round label re-apply after opt-out checks
- cleanup after first-dispatch failure

The remaining upstream review/rubrics notes are policy follow-ups rather than required branch changes:

- whether the visible label should have a separate config opt-out from automation itself
- whether later user-launched follow-up runs should default to one-shot behavior independent of the label mechanism

This PR preserves the finalized label-control behavior and leaves those broader product decisions as follow-ups.

## Validation

- `npm --prefix .agent ci`
- `npm --prefix .agent test` — passed (`378/378`)
- `git diff HEAD --check`
- Parsed workflow/action/template YAML with Ruby `YAML.load_file`
- Shell syntax checks for installed agent scripts/actions

comments.body:
## AI Review Synthesis

<!-- sepo-agent-review-synthesis -->

> Dual-agent review by **Claude** and **Codex**.
> Requested by @lolipopshock.

## Summary of PR/Issue
- This PR ports finalized orchestrator label-control behavior into Sepo by introducing a visible `agent-auto-running` label as an opt-out switch for automation chains.
- It applies the label before first dispatch, requires label presence for later-round handoffs, and removes it best-effort when orchestration stops.
- It also routes terminal review/fix-pr outcomes through orchestrator cleanup so label state is handled consistently across success/failure/unsupported paths.
- Runtime preflight now skips planner setup when heuristics already determine a terminal outcome, reducing unnecessary planner work.
- Documentation and regression coverage were updated to reflect these orchestration changes.

## Review
- Overall judgment: the core behavior is correct and largely production-ready, but two concrete engineering issues remain that are worth fixing before merge to reduce drift/regression risk.
- Reviewer disagreement: one reviewer assessed SHIP with only policy INFO notes; another identified two WARNING-level code/test issues. After deduplication, the two WARNINGs below remain actionable.

| Issue | Severity | Description |
| -- | -- | -- |
| Agent-mode heuristic gate is duplicated | WARNING | [`decideHandoff`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/dae014339fe8b0db02a476408904174893d18a1d/.agent/src/handoff.ts#L336-L353) now pre-checks heuristic dispatch, but [`decideAgentHandoff`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/dae014339fe8b0db02a476408904174893d18a1d/.agent/src/handoff.ts#L296-L334) recomputes the same heuristic gate. This creates dead/duplicated policy logic and future maintenance risk. |
| Stop-path label cleanup lacks regression coverage | WARNING | New CLI tests cover label add/check/rollback during dispatch, but not the normal `decision === "stop"` cleanup path in [`orchestrate-handoff.ts`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/dae014339fe8b0db02a476408904174893d18a1d/.agent/src/cli/orchestrate-handoff.ts#L214-L229), which is a primary operational path. |
| One-shot follow-up policy remains deferred | INFO | The label mechanism ships as intended, but the rubric/policy preference for default one-shot behavior on later user-launched follow-ups is still a separate product decision. |

## Progress
- Upstream correctness fixes called out in the PR description are present (pagination, dispatch-failure rollback, terminal cleanup routing).
- Docs were updated alongside behavior changes in `.agent/docs/*`, aligned with project conventions.
- Two inline comments were posted for the unresolved WARNING items:
  - https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/pull/7#discussion_r3155549929
  - https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/pull/7#discussion_r3155549939
- The one-shot follow-up default gap is explicitly documented as deferred policy work, not an accidental omission.

## Issue Details
<details>
<summary>Agent-mode heuristic gate is duplicated</summary>

**Cause:** `decideHandoff` now computes `heuristicDecision` and only calls `decideAgentHandoff` when heuristics already allow dispatch, but `decideAgentHandoff` still recomputes heuristic allowance internally.

**Candidate solutions:**  
1. Pass `heuristicDecision` (or `allowed`) into `decideAgentHandoff` and remove redundant recomputation/branching.  
2. Keep recomputation but explicitly refactor to a shared helper so policy-gate invariants are single-sourced and testable.

**Comments:** One reviewer treated this as non-blocking; still, removing duplicated policy checks now will reduce confusion in later orchestrator changes.
</details>

<details>
<summary>Stop-path label cleanup lacks regression coverage</summary>

**Cause:** `.agent/src/__tests__/orchestrate-handoff-cli.test.ts` validates dispatch-related label behavior but does not assert cleanup for terminal stop outcomes.

**Candidate solutions:**  
1. Add a CLI test with a terminal source outcome (`ship` or `unsupported`) and assert `DELETE .../labels/agent-auto-running` is issued on stop.  
2. Add a second variant for a missing-label/not-found cleanup case if you want to lock in best-effort semantics.

**Comments:** This gap affects continuity behavior and is a good fit for a focused regression addition.
</details>

## Recommended Next Step
`FIX_PR`: unresolved WARNING findings are concrete, code-local, and safe for an automated fix pass without product-policy ambiguity.

## Final Verdict
`MINOR_ISSUES`

## Action Items
- [ ] Refactor [`decideHandoff` / `decideAgentHandoff`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/dae014339fe8b0db02a476408904174893d18a1d/.agent/src/handoff.ts) to avoid duplicated heuristic gating and remove dead/duplicated policy logic.
- [ ] Add a regression test in [`orchestrate-handoff-cli.test.ts`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/dae014339fe8b0db02a476408904174893d18a1d/.agent/src/__tests__/orchestrate-handoff-cli.test.ts) that asserts label removal on terminal stop-path cleanup.

title: Add auto-running label control for orchestrator handoffs
